items.sentence: Olivia has made excellent ...... in maths this year.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Collocation: make progress in a subject.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "progress" forms a fixed chunk or collocation with the rest of the sentence.

wrongOptions.exam: "exam" does not match the meaning or collocation needed in this sentence — "progress" fits better.
wrongOptions.fact: "fact" does not match the meaning or collocation needed in this sentence — "progress" fits better.
wrongOptions.favour: "favour" does not match the meaning or collocation needed in this sentence — "progress" fits better.
wrongOptions.heart: "heart" does not match the meaning or collocation needed in this sentence — "progress" fits better.
wrongOptions.instance: "instance" does not match the meaning or collocation needed in this sentence — "progress" fits better.
wrongOptions.mark: "mark" does not match the meaning or collocation needed in this sentence — "progress" fits better.
wrongOptions.skill: "skill" does not match the meaning or collocation needed in this sentence — "progress" fits better.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Another chip from the box may look similar, but only one forms the natural collocation in this sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the whole sentence first, then eliminate bank words that break the meaning or collocation.

exercises.title: C: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Our swimming ...... is very friendly and helpful. (INSTRUCT)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is person who teaches swimming, so the correct answer is "instructor".

wrongOptions.instruct: The root INSTRUCT cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "instructor".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of INSTRUCT that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: INSTRUCT → instructor: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "instructor" is the word-formation answer from the root INSTRUCT in this context.

items.sentence: Atlas books are useful ...... materials. (REFER)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Atlas books are reference materials.

wrongOptions.refer: The root REFER cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "reference".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of REFER that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: REFER → reference: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "reference" is the word-formation answer from the root REFER in this context.

items.sentence: She's still a ...... at playing the violin. (BEGIN)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is someone new at an activity, so the correct answer is "beginner".

wrongOptions.begin: The root BEGIN cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "beginner".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of BEGIN that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: BEGIN → beginner: Use the -ing form for an activity or gerund.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "beginner" is the word-formation answer from the root BEGIN in this context.

items.sentence: We learnt about multiplication and ...... in today's lesson. (DIVIDE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is opposite of multiplication in maths, so the correct answer is "division".

wrongOptions.divide: The root DIVIDE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "division".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of DIVIDE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: DIVIDE → division: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "division" is the word-formation answer from the root DIVIDE in this context.

items.sentence: Two of your answers were ...... . (CORRECT)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is negative form of correct, so the correct answer is "incorrect".

wrongOptions.correct: The root CORRECT cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "incorrect".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of CORRECT that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: CORRECT → incorrect: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "incorrect" is the word-formation answer from the root CORRECT in this context.

items.sentence: I admire your ...... for speaking in front of the whole school. (BRAVE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from BRAVE, so the correct answer is "bravery".

wrongOptions.brave: The root BRAVE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "bravery".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of BRAVE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: BRAVE → bravery: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "bravery" is the word-formation answer from the root BRAVE in this context.

items.sentenceAfter: all my homework, so I can relax now.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "have done" is the only option that matches both meaning and register in this sentence.
